title: Stock Market News May 29, 2026: S&P 500 tallies 9th-straight week in the green as stocks finish May in record territory; Nasdaq up 8% for the month, Dow above 51,000
description: S&P 500 closed at 7,580.06 (+0.22%), its 21st record high of 2026 and 9th consecutive week of gains. Dow crossed 51,000 for first time, closing at 51,032.46 (+0.72%). Nasdaq Composite closed at 26,972.62 (+0.2%), surging 8% for May. All three major indexes ended the week and month in record territory.
summary: Stock Market News May 29, 2026: S&P 500 tallies 9th-straight week in the green as stocks finish May in record territory; Nasdaq up 8% for the month, Dow above 51,000

title: Oil Suffers Biggest Monthly Drop Since 2020 — Brent -19% in May
description: Brent crude futures fell 19% in May, the worst monthly decline since March 2020. WTI closed -1.73% at $87.36/bbl, Brent at $92.05. Drop driven by US-Iran ceasefire extension and Trump signaling 'final determination' on peace deal. Iran's foreign minister stated Strait of Hormuz will be open to all commercial shipping.
summary: Oil Suffers Biggest Monthly Drop Since 2020 — Brent -19% in May — Brent crude futures fell 19% in May, the worst monthly decline since March 2020

title: Dell Technologies surges 33% for its best day ever as AI server revenue soars
description: Dell (DELL) jumped 32.8% Friday — its best day on record — after Q1 beat and raising full-year guidance. Dell now sees $17.90 in adjusted EPS on $165B-$169B revenue, well above analyst expectations of $13.09/share on $142.5B. Stock up 234% in 2026. The AI infrastructure boom drove the blowout.
summary: Dell Technologies surges 33% for its best day ever as AI server revenue soars — Dell (DELL) jumped 32.8% Friday — its best day on record — after Q1 beat and raising full-year guidance

title: Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Jumps 5.4% on AI and PC Strategy News
description: Microsoft shares surged 5.4% Friday to $450.24 on volume 117% above average (77.6M shares). Driven by Nvidia-Microsoft Computex tease of 'a new era of PC,' new coding model announcements at Build, and strong earnings backdrop (Q1 EPS $4.27 vs $4.06 est, revenue $82.89B, +18.3% YoY). Consensus analyst target $560.88 (39 Buy, 7 Hold).
summary: Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Jumps 5.4% on AI and PC Strategy News — Microsoft shares surged 5.4% Friday to $450.24 on volume 117% above average (77.6M shares)

title: Meta Stock Jumps 4% As It Launches Premium Facebook, Instagram Subscriptions
description: Meta announced 'Meta One' premium subscription plans: Instagram Plus $3.99/mo, Facebook Plus $3.99/mo, WhatsApp Plus $2.99/mo, and AI-powered tiers up to $19.99/mo. Shares rose 3.7% to $635.36. Comes amid $125-145B AI capex for 2026 (doubled from 2025) and 15,000 workers reassigned/cut for AI integration. Stock down ~4% YTD.
summary: Meta Stock Jumps 4% As It Launches Premium Facebook, Instagram Subscriptions — Meta announced 'Meta One' premium subscription plans: Instagram Plus $3.99/mo, Facebook Plus $3.99/mo, WhatsApp Plus $2.99/mo, and AI-powered tiers up to $19.99/mo

title: Warren Buffett's Successor Greg Abel Triples Berkshire's Alphabet Stake While Hedge Fund Billionaires Sell
description: Berkshire Hathaway more than tripled its Alphabet (GOOGL) position in Q1 2026 under new CEO Greg Abel, now 6.7% of the $332B portfolio. Meanwhile, Bill Ackman eliminated his stake to buy MSFT, and Stanley Druckenmiller exited completely. Alphabet trades at ~30x P/E, $4.6T market cap. Abel's move signals long-term AI conviction contrasting with hedge fund short-term rotation.
summary: Warren Buffett's Successor Greg Abel Triples Berkshire's Alphabet Stake While Hedge Fund Billionaires Sell — Berkshire Hathaway more than tripled its Alphabet (GOOGL) position in Q1 2026 under new CEO Greg Abel, now 6.7% of the $332B portfolio

title: An Iran Deal Could Actually Trigger a Painful Stock-Market Selloff, Despite Wall Street's Optimism
description: Nomura strategist Charlie McElligott warns a US-Iran peace deal could catalyze a selloff rather than a rally. The S&P 500 is up 15.9% since late March, Nasdaq +24.5%, driven by gamma squeezes and leverage. A deal would lower rates and the dollar, triggering rotation out of overweight tech into beaten-down cyclicals. Median S&P 500 short interest at 3% of market cap — highest since 2011 (Goldman data). VIX at 15.32.
summary: An Iran Deal Could Actually Trigger a Painful Stock-Market Selloff, Despite Wall Street's Optimism — Nomura strategist Charlie McElligott warns a US-Iran peace deal could catalyze a selloff rather than a rally

title: US Consumer Confidence Declines in May — Inflation & War Worries Mount
description: Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index fell 0.7 points to 93.1 in May, driven by gas prices up 50%+ since Iran war. 'Jobs plentiful' at lowest since Feb 2021. Two-thirds of consumers cutting back spending. Trump approval at near-lowest since Jan 2025. Fed expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% into 2027. JOBS HARD TO GET at 7-month low — mixed signal.
summary: US Consumer Confidence Declines in May — Inflation & War Worries Mount — Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index fell 0.7 points to 93.1 in May, driven by gas prices up 50%+ since Iran war

title: AAII Investor Sentiment Diverges — Bears Rise to 43% as Markets Hit Records
description: AAII bearish sentiment rose to 43% (from 36%), bullish fell to 31% (lowest since March), bull-bear spread in double digits favoring bears for first time since early April. Divergence: major indexes hit all-time highs while individual investors grew more cautious. Neutral below 30%. Question: will peace deal optimism reverse this or will divergence persist?
summary: AAII Investor Sentiment Diverges — Bears Rise to 43% as Markets Hit Records — AAII bearish sentiment rose to 43% (from 36%), bullish fell to 31% (lowest since March), bull-bear spread in double digits favoring bears for first time since early April

title: S&P Global Cuts 2026 Global Growth Forecast to 2.2% — 'Mounting Economic Costs of Middle East Conflict'
description: S&P Global slashed 2026 global GDP forecast to 2.2% from 2.9% in February. Brent crude expected above $100/b through 2026, annual average ~100% higher than pre-conflict estimates. Fed first rate cut pushed to mid-2027. PMI input costs saw largest two-month jump in over a decade. US equities resilient but tech vs. rest divergence stark. Consumer spending under threat from real income squeeze.
summary: S&P Global Cuts 2026 Global Growth Forecast to 2.2% — 'Mounting Economic Costs of Middle East Conflict' — S&P Global slashed 2026 global GDP forecast to 2.2% from 2.9% in February

title: JP Morgan Weekly Market Recap: S&P 500 at 7,473, Small Caps Outperforming, Energy +33.3% YTD
description: Data as of May 25: S&P 500 at 7,473 (YTD +9.69%), Dow 50,580 (+5.89%), Nasdaq 26,344 (+13.62%), Russell 2000 7,131 (+16.10%). Energy best sector YTD at +33.3%. 10yr Treasury 4.56%, 2yr 4.13%, SOFR 3.51%. WTI crude $96.75. Gold $4,506/oz. Silver $75.84. Copper $13,545. Dollar: $1.16 per EUR, ¥159.12 per USD.
summary: JP Morgan Weekly Market Recap: S&P 500 at 7,473, Small Caps Outperforming, Energy +33.3% YTD — Data as of May 25: S&P 500 at 7,473 (YTD +9.69%), Dow 50,580 (+5.89%), Nasdaq 26,344 (+13.62%), Russell 2000 7,131 (+16.10%)

title: Fidelity Midyear Outlook: Bull Market Intact but Oil Supply Shock Is the Key Risk
description: 84% of S&P 500 companies beat Q1 profit estimates. Revenue growth ~10%, operating margins at all-time high ~16%. Magnificent 7 mega-caps projected ~$700B in AI data center spending for 2026. But crude oil carries a 60% 'scarcity premium' — Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic collapsed from 102/week to 4/week. Fidelity's Jurrien Timmer 'more concerned about downside risk than upside potential.' Recommends energy, tech, financials sectors.
summary: Fidelity Midyear Outlook: Bull Market Intact but Oil Supply Shock Is the Key Risk — 84% of S&P 500 companies beat Q1 profit estimates

title: Apple Hits Fresh All-Time Highs — Market Cap $4.62T, Up 15% in May
description: Apple (AAPL) hit new intraday highs in 16 of May's 20 trading days, reaching ~$313. Market cap $4.62T as of May 30. Record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $111.18B with iPhone revenue $56.99B driven by extraordinary iPhone 17 demand. Up ~15% in May, +56% past year.
summary: Apple Hits Fresh All-Time Highs — Market Cap $4.62T, Up 15% in May — Apple (AAPL) hit new intraday highs in 16 of May's 20 trading days, reaching ~$313

title: Tesla Stock Down 1% YTD vs S&P 500 +10% — Three Catalysts That Could Turn It Around
description: Tesla (TSLA) YTD -1% while S&P 500 +10%. Q1 revenue $22.4B (+16% YoY). Capex raised to over $25B. Trades at 208x forward earnings. Potential H2 catalysts: Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot reveal (July/August), robotaxi expansion (Dallas, Houston live), and unsupervised FSD potentially ready Q4 2026. SpaceX IPO looms as Musk engagement risk.
summary: Tesla Stock Down 1% YTD vs S&P 500 +10% — Three Catalysts That Could Turn It Around — Tesla (TSLA) YTD -1% while S&P 500 +10%

title: Amazon Deepens AI Shopping Push — Alexa for Shopping Launched, Ad Revenue Hits $70B TTM
description: AMZN merged Rufus and Alexa+ into free 'Alexa for Shopping.' 300M+ customers used Rufus in 2025 driving $12B incremental sales. AWS Agentic Shopping Assistant converts 3.5x better than keyword search. Q1 ad revenue $17.2B (+24% YoY), TTM $70B+. But free cash flow collapsed to $1.2B (from $25.9B) on $59.3B AI infrastructure spend. Zacks rates Hold, forward P/E 29.39x. Stock +17.1% in 6 months.
summary: Amazon Deepens AI Shopping Push — Alexa for Shopping Launched, Ad Revenue Hits $70B TTM — AMZN merged Rufus and Alexa+ into free 'Alexa for Shopping.' 300M+ customers used Rufus in 2025 driving $12B incremental sales

title: Jamie Dimon 'Cautiously Pessimistic' on Economy as JPMorgan Stock Hits Records
description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon expressed 'cautious pessimism' in a Fox Business interview on May 29 despite markets at record highs. JPM closed at $296.40 on May 29, down from $308.49 on May 26. Dimon's caution contrasts with market euphoria. JPMorgan raised 2026 global equity outlook forecasting double-digit gains.
summary: Jamie Dimon 'Cautiously Pessimistic' on Economy as JPMorgan Stock Hits Records — JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon expressed 'cautious pessimism' in a Fox Business interview on May 29 despite markets at record highs

title: Broadcom Reports June 3 — Oppenheimer Predicts Beat-and-Raise on AI Demand
description: Broadcom (AVGO) reports earnings June 3. Oppenheimer predicts beat-and-raise driven by AI ASIC and networking demand from hyperscale customers. Stock up 82% over past year. $10B share buyback authorized through December 2026. Part of the AI infrastructure theme driving semis — PHLX Semiconductor Index up over 70% since late March.
summary: Broadcom Reports June 3 — Oppenheimer Predicts Beat-and-Raise on AI Demand — Broadcom (AVGO) reports earnings June 3

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Keynote in Taipei June 1 — 'New Era of PC' Teased
description: Jensen Huang keynote at Computex Taipei June 1 (May 31 11PM EDT). Nvidia and Microsoft teasing 'a new era of PC' with potential AI-focused chip announcements. NVDA raised dividend 2,400% after record Q1 earnings. Options market priced $355 billion market cap swing around earnings. Stock remains center of AI infrastructure narrative.
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Keynote in Taipei June 1 — 'New Era of PC' Teased — Jensen Huang keynote at Computex Taipei June 1 (May 31 11PM EDT)

title: Trading Week Ahead: The Warsh Trade Meets Its First Data Test
description: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair triggered hawkish repricing. Core PCE + GDP data on May 28 were first test. Iran peace breakthrough drove oil down ~7% on the week. Current setup: WTI $87.36 after -19% May decline. Next week's key: ISM Manufacturing PMI Monday, ISM Services Wednesday, Jobs Report Friday. Fed funds futures now pricing higher chance of hike than cut in 2027.
summary: Trading Week Ahead: The Warsh Trade Meets Its First Data Test — Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair triggered hawkish repricing

title: Credit Markets: 2026 Marks Return of Buyer's Market as AI Hyperscaler Issuance Surges
description: Apollo Global: credit markets shifted to buyer's market in 2026 driven by AI-related hyperscaler issuance and M&A reacceleration. IG yields near three-year lows but supply surging. Carlyle: Q1-2026 funding liquidity shock could mark credit cycle turn, with wider spreads improving investment opportunity set. Private credit lending reached $3 trillion, exceeding public HY and broadly syndicated loan markets combined.
summary: Credit Markets: 2026 Marks Return of Buyer's Market as AI Hyperscaler Issuance Surges — Apollo Global: credit markets shifted to buyer's market in 2026 driven by AI-related hyperscaler issuance and M&A reacceleration

title: Commodities Outlook: World Bank Sees 24% Energy Price Surge in 2026
description: World Bank projects energy prices surging 24% in 2026 to highest since Russia's 2022 Ukraine invasion, driven by Middle East war. Deutsche Bank forecasts 2026 average Brent $61.5/b (pre-conflict view). Gold at $4,570, silver $75.84, copper $13,545 — all near multi-year highs. BMO Commodities Outlook for May flags upward revisions to crude, refined products, natural gas forecasts.
summary: Commodities Outlook: World Bank Sees 24% Energy Price Surge in 2026 — World Bank projects energy prices surging 24% in 2026 to highest since Russia's 2022 Ukraine invasion, driven by Middle East war

title: Micron up 88% in May, Qualcomm +40% — AI Memory and Chip Boom
description: Micron (MU) rose 5% Friday, up ~88% in May. Qualcomm (QCOM) +3% Friday, +40% in May. Samsung surged 6.5% after shipping HBM4E AI memory chips. PHLX Semiconductor Index up over 70% since late March, driven by high-bandwidth memory supply bottleneck. 14 S&P 500 stocks at all-time highs Friday including Broadcom, Micron, Qualcomm, CrowdStrike, Datadog.
summary: Micron up 88% in May, Qualcomm +40% — AI Memory and Chip Boom — Micron (MU) rose 5% Friday, up ~88% in May

title: Solar ETF Posts Best Month Since 2013 — Enphase +110%, SolarEdge +79% in May
description: Solar ETF (TAN) surged ~26.5% in May, best month since September 2013. Enphase Energy (ENPH) +110% in May, SolarEdge (SEDG) +79%. Rally driven by peace deal optimism reducing oil prices and refocusing on energy transition. Clean energy sector benefiting from rotation out of traditional energy names.
summary: Solar ETF Posts Best Month Since 2013 — Enphase +110%, SolarEdge +79% in May — Solar ETF (TAN) surged ~26.5% in May, best month since September 2013

title: University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Plummets to Record Lows in May
description: UMich Consumer Sentiment final May reading: 44.8, down from 49.8 preliminary and 52.2 in April (-10.0% MoM). Current Economic Conditions: 45.8 (-12.8% MoM). Index of Consumer Expectations plunged, reflecting inflation fears from Iran war and tariff impacts. Sharpest decline concentrated in lower-income households.
summary: University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Plummets to Record Lows in May — UMich Consumer Sentiment final May reading: 44.8, down from 49.8 preliminary and 52.2 in April (-10.0% MoM)

title: Space Stocks Tumble After Blue Origin Rocket Ground-Test Explosion
description: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) -14.5%, EchoStar (SATS) -4%, Rocket Lab (RKLB) -4.5% on Friday after Blue Origin rocket suffered a ground-test explosion. The incident raised safety concerns across the commercial space sector. Comes amid broader space industry consolidation and competition from SpaceX.
summary: Space Stocks Tumble After Blue Origin Rocket Ground-Test Explosion — AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) -14.5%, EchoStar (SATS) -4%, Rocket Lab (RKLB) -4.5% on Friday after Blue Origin rocket suffered a ground-test explosion

title: Gap Plunges 14% After Cutting FY Sales Outlook — Consumer Discretionary Warning
description: Gap (GAP) dropped 14% Friday after cutting FY sales growth outlook to 1-2%. Q1 same-store sales +2% vs +3.1% expected. American Eagle (AEO) also fell 11% after comp sales -2% vs +3.1% expected. Signals consumer spending pressure in apparel retail, consistent with Conference Board data showing two-thirds of consumers cutting back.
summary: Gap Plunges 14% After Cutting FY Sales Outlook — Consumer Discretionary Warning — Gap (GAP) dropped 14% Friday after cutting FY sales growth outlook to 1-2%

title: Eli Lilly Gains 4% — CVS Caremark Agrees to Cover Zepbound, Barclays Target $1,400
description: Eli Lilly (LLY) gained 4% Thursday after CVS Caremark agreed to cover Zepbound weight-loss drug. Barclays raised price target to $1,400 implying 24% upside. Obesity drug market continues to expand as more insurers add coverage. LLY market cap among top pharma names.
summary: Eli Lilly Gains 4% — CVS Caremark Agrees to Cover Zepbound, Barclays Target $1,400 — Eli Lilly (LLY) gained 4% Thursday after CVS Caremark agreed to cover Zepbound weight-loss drug

title: Fed Rate Hike Calls Emerge — Former Trump Economist Suggests 100bps This Year
description: Joe Lavorgna (SMBC Americas, former Trump economist) argued for 100bps of rate hikes in 2026 citing tight credit spreads, record equities, and no tightening of financial conditions. Core PCE annual rate at peak since Nov 2023. Three- and six-month inflation trends moving toward 4%. Polymarket odds now favor hold at 3.50-3.75% through year-end with hike risk priced.

title: Japan Nikkei Surges 2.5%, South Korea Kospi Hits Record — Asia Markets Rally
description: Nikkei 225 +2.53% to 66,329.5. Topix +1.41% to new record 3,957.17. Kospi +3% to fresh intraday record 8,476.15, driven by Samsung +6.5% on HBM4E AI memory chip shipments. ASX 200 +1.62%. Global equity rally broad-based, with semiconductor theme driving Asian tech names.

title: Kevin Warsh Era Begins — Hawkish Fed Chair Confirmed, First Data Test Passed
description: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair. His confirmation triggered immediate hawkish repricing: long-end yields up, USD strengthened, risk assets initially reversed. Core PCE and GDP data on May 28 largely validated the hawkish stance. Warsh faces Iran peace deal disinflation vs. sticky core inflation dilemma. Market pricing first cut now mid-2027 at earliest.
summary: Kevin Warsh Era Begins — Hawkish Fed Chair Confirmed, First Data Test Passed — Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair
